content.text: Four ways to spend a career. The ledger prices them the same.

Past the day-to-day — ammo, repairs, the creditor's tithe (see The Creditor Table) — what's left of a career flows toward a flagship investment. There are four, and they sit on one scale. You will not afford them all. Pick what your career becomes.

rows: the training ladder and the access funnel — see Stats & Growth
the install ladder and the tier chassis — see Trees Overview
PP, tiers, and the one-Name rule — see Professions Overview
the raise, the bond, and the alternative of buying one trained — see Influence

content.text: The 7,800-credit figure is the printed sum of the training ladder from 4 to 10; the rung-by-rung prices and the downtime each rung takes live on Stats & Growth, along with the band past 10, which is a different economy entirely. The mastery figure is the printed sum of the install ladder; the per-tier prices live on Trees Overview.

The table is priced in two currencies, and the anchors lean differently. The stat and the mastery are bought mostly in credits — mission pay, after the creditor takes its half. The Name is bought almost entirely in calendar — dedicated downtime periods that could have been anything else. The war-beast costs like mastery, and whether you pay the Wrangler's way (raise it) or the Fixer's way (buy it trained) is its own build choice — see Influence.
The stat makes everything you already do better — every roll it touches, for the rest of the career.
The mastery finishes a tree: the full chassis, the capstones, the ceiling the tree sells. (What each ceiling is — see Trees Overview.)
The Name makes you someone in the economy — one per character, and it travels with you. (See Professions Overview.)
The beast is a second activation on the field with its own rolls and its own treadmill. (See Influence.)

An anchor is a campaign-length purchase. It lands in the late acts of a career, not the early ones — which is exactly why the choice is worth making on purpose, early, while the credits and the calendar still have somewhere to go.

content.label: ONE SCALE
content.text: All four anchors price out to roughly the same career. The question is never which is cheapest — it's which one ends up with your name on it.

RETIREMENT PLANNING NOTICE: Contractors are encouraged to designate a primary capital development track at onboarding. Contractors who decline to designate will find one designated for them by circumstance, at circumstance's usual rates.
